subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Boiled Potato — Tangent Line Approx, Concavity & Separation of Variables (Alternate DE)
stimulus: At time $t = 0$, a boiled potato is taken from a pot on a stove and left to cool in a kitchen. The internal temperature of the potato is 91 degrees Celsius (°C) at time $t = 0$, and the internal temperature of the potato is greater than 27 °C for all times $t > 0$. The internal temperature of the potato at time $t$ minutes can be modeled by the function $H$ that satisfies the differential equation $\frac{dH}{dt} = -\frac{1}{4}(H - 27)$, where $H(t)$ is measured in degrees Celsius and $H(0) = 91$.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $H$ at $t = 0$. Use this equation to approximate the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Slope $= -\frac{1}{4}(91-27) = -16$. Tangent line: $y = 91 - 16t$. Approximation at $t = 3$: $91 - 48 = 43$ degrees Celsius.

parts.prompt: Use $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2}$ to determine whether your answer in part (a) is an underestimate or an overestimate of the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} = \frac{1}{16}(H-27) > 0$ for $H > 27$. The graph of $H$ is concave up, so the tangent line is below the curve, making part (a) an underestimate.

alternatives.correct_example: $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} = -\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{dH}{dt} = -\frac{1}{4} \cdot \left(-\frac{1}{4}(H-27)\right) = \frac{1}{16}(H-27) > 0$ for $H > 27$; since $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} > 0$, the graph of $H$ is concave up, so the tangent line lies below the graph, making the estimate an underestimate

parts.prompt: For $t < 10$, an alternate model for the internal temperature of the potato at time $t$ minutes is the function $G$ that satisfies the differential equation $\frac{dG}{dt} = -(G - 27)^{2/3}$, where $G(t)$ is measured in degrees Celsius and $G(0) = 91$. Find an expression for $G(t)$. Based on this model, what is the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Separation $\Rightarrow$ $3(G-27)^{1/3} = -t + C$. $C = 12$ from $G(0)=91$. $G(t) = 27 + \left(\frac{12-t}{3}\right)^3$. $G(3) = 27 + (9/3)^3 = 54$ degrees Celsius. 0/5 if no separation of variables; max 2/5 if no constant of integration.

scoring_points.description: Includes constant of integration and uses initial condition $G(0) = 91$
